name: Marcus_Mobile_Plumber
description: 38-year-old plumbing company owner. Runs Marcus Plumbing LLC solo — fixes burst pipes, installs water heaters, clears drains. Uses QuickBooks on his phone between jobs to send estimates and invoices. High stress, always in a hurry, fat fingers from physical work. Hates paperwork but knows he needs to get paid.

name: Diane_Office_Manager
description: 45-year-old office manager at Marcus Plumbing LLC. Handles scheduling, client follow-ups, and billing when Marcus is on a job. Uses QuickBooks Online on her desktop daily — sends invoices, records payments, and chases outstanding balances. Steady and methodical but gets frustrated when workflows don't match how she thinks.

name: Sofia_Event_Organizer
description: 34-year-old founder of Stellar Events LLC. Plans weddings, corporate parties, and birthday events. Gets 5-10 email inquiries a day and needs to turn them into proposals fast. Uses QuickBooks for estimates and invoices, Gmail for client communication. Creative and social but finds accounting software unintuitive — she tracks value in relationships, not ledgers.

name: Rachel_Event_Assistant
description: 27-year-old event coordination assistant at Stellar Events LLC. Handles inbound email inquiries, drafts initial estimates for Sofia to review, and coordinates with vendors. First real job out of hospitality school. Confident with email but nervous about sending wrong quotes in QuickBooks — checks everything twice before hitting send.

name: Carlos_CPA
description: 42-year-old CPA who runs Greenfield Tax & Advisory and manages 30+ small business clients. Expert in tax, bookkeeping, and advisory. Heavy QuickBooks power user — runs P&L, reviews bank feeds, processes payroll, and sends invoices for his own billable hours. Impatient with slow or cluttered interfaces. Expects software to get out of his way.

name: Martha_Tax_Assistant
description: 58-year-old bookkeeper assistant at Greenfield Tax & Advisory. Has used desktop QuickBooks for 20 years and handles bookkeeping for Carlos's smaller clients. Very comfortable with invoices, P&L reports, and bank reconciliations but intimidated by new web features and popup dialogs she hasn't seen before. Slow and deliberate — she never rushes.
